title: Navigation Mechanism Acceptance Standard
purpose: Define the minimum gate between candidate route learning, validated route learning, accepted mechanism affordance, projected entry behavior, and observed future behavior.

state_model.meaning: A trace-derived or provider-suggested claim is visible as route-learning evidence but cannot mutate owner artifacts.
The claim passed local schema, verb, provider-authority, and replay checks but is not owner-accepted.
A validated claim has a recorded transaction blocker before owner acceptance.
A non-authoritative packet asks owner surfaces for proof, materialization target, rejection, or reroute.
A candidate owner code/tool locus has been verified, but the acceptance gate has not yet promoted it to owner acceptance.
The claim has been submitted to the owner surface for an explicit acceptance decision.
The owner standard or owner tool accepted the claim for downstream materialization.
An owner tool materialized the accepted claim into a mechanism, standard, skill, WorkItem, or entry surface.
Future trace evidence shows the projected route behavior changed or improved.

forbidden: accepted claim without owner standard/tool acceptance proof
direct canonical mutation from a candidate projection

acceptance_transaction_spine.purpose: Append-only transaction events are the source of truth for navigation mechanism acceptance state; dossiers, candidate cards, metabolism counters, and future observation queues are read-model projections.
acceptance_transaction_spine.blocked_event_rule: acceptance.blocked.recorded may improve blocker provenance and next legal events, but it must not increment accepted_count, projected_count, or observed_count.
acceptance_transaction_spine.owner_packet_event_rule: owner_packet.created must be appended as a transaction event after a blocked event, use authority_posture owner_packet_not_owner_acceptance, reference the owner packet in proof_refs.owner_packet_ref, and must not increment accepted_count, projected_count, or observed_count.
acceptance_transaction_spine.owner_locus_event_rule: owner_locus.verified_candidate may record candidate code/tool loci after owner_packet.created, but it uses authority_posture owner_locus_candidate_not_owner_acceptance and must not increment accepted_count, projected_count, or observed_count.
acceptance_transaction_spine.owner_acceptance_request_event_rule: owner_acceptance.requested may be appended after owner_locus.verified_candidate only when proof_refs.replay_ref resolves to a durable replay receipt. It is a request event, not owner acceptance, and must keep owner_acceptance_ref, projection_ref, and observation_ref null.
acceptance_transaction_spine.observation_event_rule: observation.recorded may be appended after projection.recorded only when proof_refs.observation_ref identifies a replayable post-projection trace window, the event carries observation_proof authority, missing_refs is empty, and post_count is lower than baseline_count for the declared metric.
acceptance_transaction_spine.observed_debt_retirement_rule: When a claim reaches observed, navigation metabolism must retire the matching process-audit anti-pattern from active behavior_debt and top_repairs while preserving the row as advisory evidence until a later regression event reopens it. Downstream enforcement surfaces such as coverage_enforcement_matrix and navigation_index_spine must carry active_debt/advisory lifecycle fields so retired rows are not reissued as active repair targets.
acceptance_transaction_spine.source_authority_rule: The event stream owns transaction state. Dossiers and cards may expose latest event refs, but they do not become transaction authority.

owner_packet.purpose: Ask the relevant owner surfaces whether a candidate route repair is already embodied, should be materialized, should be rejected, or should be rerouted.
required_questions: Does existing code already embody this repair?
If not embodied, what minimal owner-owned materialization is acceptable?
What trace window proves improvement after projection?

owner_locus_verification.purpose: Record local evidence that existing owner code/tool loci embody a candidate repair, without promoting that evidence to owner acceptance.
owner_locus_verification.non_authority_rule: Owner-locus verification can satisfy candidate code/tool-loci evidence but cannot satisfy owner_acceptance_ref, projection_ref, or observation_ref.

durable_replay_receipt.purpose: Persist a passed route replay result so request events and read models can resolve replay evidence from disk instead of relying on an ephemeral command result.
durable_replay_receipt.non_acceptance_rule: A durable replay receipt may remove durable_replay_receipt_lane_unresolved from missing_refs, but it must not set acceptance_eligibility=accepted or increment accepted_count, projected_count, or observed_count.

authority_boundary.transaction_event: Records state movement and blockers, not owner acceptance by itself.
authority_boundary.blocked_dossier: Read-model projection over the latest blocked transaction state.
authority_boundary.owner_packet: Request packet to owner surfaces, not acceptance proof.
authority_boundary.durable_replay_receipt: Durable replay evidence only; satisfies the replay receipt lane but not owner acceptance.
authority_boundary.owner_acceptance_proof: Explicit owner standard/tool acceptance ref plus code/tool loci proof.
authority_boundary.observation_proof: Post-projection trace window or equivalent replayable observation evidence.

non_authoritative_surfaces.transform_job_receipts: Provider work evidence only; may not satisfy owner_acceptance_ref.
non_authoritative_surfaces.row_patches: Materialization candidates only; may not satisfy owner_acceptance_ref.
non_authoritative_surfaces.compliance_ledger: Compliance evidence only; may not satisfy owner_acceptance_ref.
non_authoritative_surfaces.route_replay_result: Fitness/conformance evidence only; may not satisfy owner_acceptance_ref.
non_authoritative_surfaces.navigation_mechanism_replay_receipt: Durable replay evidence only; may not satisfy owner_acceptance_ref, projection_ref, or observation_ref.

acceptance_dossier.purpose: Project why one validated route-learning claim is still blocked before owner acceptance.

forbidden: accept candidate navigation mechanisms from compliance findings
mutate acceptance events or dossiers from the scanner
duplicate the owner validator acceptance rules

compliance_coverage.atlas_navigation_use: Atlas and navigation consume this standard through the generated compliance_ledger row after build_compliance_ledger.py runs; navigation-mechanism acceptance state remains owned by the event stream, dossiers, and owner validator.
